Petition For Instructions Concerning Petitioner'S Objections To Statutory And Extraordinary Fees Requested By Attorneys
Off Calendar Aug-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Herp for and with Raul Menjivar. Michelle Menjivar, Marilyn Menjivar and Ricardo Menjivar also present.

The Court stated that we are here for an amended final report, an amended petition for instructions regarding extraordinary fees, that objections have been filed, that there is an issue of unauthorized preliminary distributions, that it took 15 years to close the estate and that there are extraordinary fees in the amount of $22,600.00 for various tasks including tax reports, sale of real property and defending the request for extraordinary fees. Ms. Herp gave a history of the case, the work performed, that when it came time to sell the real property the administrator stopped communicating with her firm, that the delay was not caused by her firm and that they should not be penalized, that Mr. Menjivar is objecting to fees and the fee schedule, cited P.C. 7.707 regarding the fee schedule, that the extraordinary fees are broken down into 4 categories and they are tax returns, alleged violations of building ordnances, sale of property and fees due to the challenge of the fee request, that in 2008 her firm was contacted regarding the building violations, that in 2012 they resumed the case at the courts request, that they contacted Mr. Menjivar regarding the issues with the property, that it seemed that he would cooperate, that they notified Mr. Menjivar in writing regarding the extraordinary fees and that all work was beneficial to the estate and the fees were well earned. The Court stated that the objection says that Mr. Menjivar contacted the agent and title company regarding the real property but the firm billed for this. Ms. Herp stated that given the situation it would have been a dereliction of her duty not to check in with the broker and title company to be kept informed. Mr. Menjivar stated that the broker handled everything including guiding him through the repairs and the sale, that he provided Ms. Herp with information, that her pursuing the information should not be at his cost, that he received notice of fee rates over the years but that it was not his responsibility to keep track of them and that he wants a fair evaluation of the fees. The Court reminded Mr. Menjivar that the case went on for many years due to him not doing his job as administrator. Mr. Menjivar stated that Ms. Herp continued to work even though he told her not to and that she failed to inform him of all of the fees. Michelle Menjivar stated that they do owe fees, that they feel there are discrepancies, that it was made clear during the sale that the Menjivar's wanted minimun attorney involvment, that the work on the house was done with Nick Cola not the law firm and that they had a good relationship with Mr. Cola. Ricardo Menjivar stated that he would like everything to be fair. Marilyn Menjivar stated that they worked with the City Attorney's office, that Ms. Herp told them that she had other clients and could no longer help them and that they were not told when the extraordinary fees would kick in. Ms. Herp stated that everyone did a lot of work, that the City Attorney contacted her office, that they had exhausted efforts to work with the administrator, that at that point Michelle became more involved and Ms. Herp kept her involvment to a minimun and that the preliminary distribution was made because 2 of the beneficiaries had urgent need of funds. The accounting petition and the Petition for instructions, etc., were taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-23-2015 Continued To Jul-07-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Herp for (executor) Raul Menjivar.

Ms. Herp reported that Mr. Menjivar is in the hospital, that he wanted to be present today and confirmed that an objection has not been filed. The accounting petition and the Petition for Instructions, etc., were continued to July 7, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court instructed Ms. Herp to remind Mr. Menjivar that if he plans to object to the petitions he must file the documents prior to the next hearing.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Instructions Concerning Petitioner'S Objections To Statutory And Extraordinary Fees Requested By Attorneys
Off Calendar Jun-23-2015 Continued To Jul-07-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-09-2015 Continued To Jun-23-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Herp for and with (executor) Raul Menjivar. Michelle & Marilyn Menjivar (sisters/heirs) also present.

The Court stated that an amended petition was filed on June 2, 2015 but that it does not appear additional notice was given. Ms. Herp reported that she filed further notice, that courtesy copies were delivered, that she was not aware additional notice needed to be given, that the change was withdrawing the extraordinary fees from the accounting petition and adding it to the petition for instructions where notice was given. Mr. Menjivar stated that he received notice yesterday, that he has not had time to review it, that he has concerns regarding the $44,000.00 in statutory fees and that he objects to the proceeding going forward today. The Court stated that as Mr. Menjivar has not had the statutory period to review the documents the accounting petition and the petition for instructions, etc., was continued to June 23, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If there are objections to the amended petition they must be submitted in writing prior to the new hearing date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Further Status On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Feb-24-2015 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Further Status On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Jan-27-2015 Continued To Feb-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Herp for and with (executor) Raul Menjivar. Michelle Menjivar (beneificary) also present.

Ms. Herp reported that on January 22, 2015 she received the information to prepare the petition for final distribution, on January 24, 2015 it was sent to Mr. Menjivar for review and signing of verification, that he is refusing to sign and objects to the attorney's fees. Ms. Menjivar stated that they have made great progress in moving forward, they would like to close but have serious issues with regard to the fees. Mr. Menjivar stated that he received the petition late and has not had enough time to review it. The court instructed Mr. Menjivar to get the petition on file with the verification and suggested that if he is not happy with his current counsel he should obtain new representation. Ms. Herp stated that they are close to the end, ready to distribute, will consider filing a petition for instructions along with the accounting petition and requests a short continuance. The further status on status of administration was continued to February 24, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that the petitions will be filed by that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Park. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Sep-30-2014 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieves As Counsel
Off Calendar Jul-08-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Herp for and with Raul Menjivar.

Mr. Menjivar stated that the house has been listed, that he signed the listing agreement with the Broker yesterday, that the house is vacant and that it is insured. Ms. Herp stated that the house will be listed at $755,000.00 within two weeks, that she is happy to proceed with this and continue to represent the Executor, and that she withdraws her motion to be relieved without prejudice to re-filing if needed. Mr. Menjivar requested that Ms. Herp not be involved with the sale of the property. The Court informed Mr. Menjivar that it will not limit Ms. Herp's representation and that she will be needed for review of the sales documents. The Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel is off calendar. Status of administration on calendar for September 30, 2014 will remain on calendar for status on sale of the property as well.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition For Allowance Of Interum Statutory Compensation To Attorneys, For Extraordinary Attorneys' Fees And For Reimbursement Of Costs Advanced.
Off Calendar May-23-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Herp for and with Raul Menjivar (Executor). Michelle Menjivar (Heir) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Menjivar stated that they are going to sell the property, that he is meeting with the Realtor this Friday, and assured the Court and Ms. Herp that the house is going to be listed and sold. Ms. Herp explained circumstances and services provided to support the request for extraordinary fees. The Parties explained difficulties/challenges with regard to the Attorney Client relationship and addressed Mr. Menjivar's objections. The Court advised Ms. Herp that it is not inclined to allow Mr. Menjivar to remain unrepresented. Status of administration was continued to June 10, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see if the house is listed, and to see if new Counsel is coming in or if Mr. Menjivar is going to proceed with Ms. Herp. The Petition for Allowance of Interim Statutory Compensation, etc. was taken under submission. The Court stated that it is inclined to allow some statutory fees and review extraordinary fees at conclusion of the Estate.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Jones. (Not Reported)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar May-14-2014 Continued To Jun-10-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Mar-19-2014 Continued To May-14-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Allowance Of Interum Statutory Compensation To Attorneys, For Extraordinary Attorneys' Fees And For Reimbursement Of Costs Advanced.
Off Calendar Mar-19-2014 Continued To May-14-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Herp for and with Raul Menjivar.

The Court stated that this estate needs to be closed, that Mr. Menjivar needs to sell the property now or distribute it to himself and his siblings, that the law provides the estate to close within eighteen months and that it can't keep the estate open. Ms. Herp stated that it has been her desire to close/distribute the estate as well, that they are going in circles, and that she has been asked to not file a petition for final distribution and withdraw. The Petition for Allowance of Interim Statutory Compensation, etc. and status of the accounting were continued to May 14, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court explained to Mr. Menjivar that if he does neither of the above, that it is going to remove him. Mr. Menjivar indicated that he will dispute the request for extraordinary fees. Mr. Menjivar was directed to file his objection.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator and the Attorney to appear on January 21, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the report on status of administration has not been filed. The status hearing was also continued to that date.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Sep-17-2013 Continued To Dec-10-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Herp for Raul Menjivar (Executor).

Ms. Herp stated that the work on the property (Rhode Island Street) was completed pursuant to the permit, that the work was inspected and passed inspection on July 24, 2013, and that her Client continues working on the property cleaning up, removing debries, removing vegetation, etc. Ms. Herp also stated that a listing agreement was signed for the Rhode Island street property at $649,000.00 (as is condition), that it will be marketed at the end of the month, that all of the Siblings are participating in the clean up of the property, that Marilyn (Daughter of Decedent) has a life estate in the Cayuga Street property and that the two Clearlake properties were lost in foreclosure four years ago. Status on filing of a petition for final distribution was continued to December 10, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)
